mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 60% of months when RSAFS increases month-over-month, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A high and rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor market weakness, reducing consumer income and spending, thereby depressing corporate profits and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in over half of the instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is near zero and unemployment (UNRATE) remains elevated, the economy is in a liquidity trap where conventional monetary stimulus is exhausted, limiting equity upside and actual rate increases would be especially harmful.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with high unemployment
failure_condition: If the forward 60-day return is positive in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and UNRATE > 8%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a low-interest-rate regime (FEDFUNDS below 1%), rising housing starts (HOUST) signal a robust recovery in the housing sector and broader economy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns as construction activity and consumer wealth increase.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of observations when HOUST rises month-over-month and FEDFUNDS < 1%, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above a low threshold signals emerging inflationary pressures that may prompt the Fed to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than half of the months where CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates synchronized strength in employment and manufacturing output, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If the forward 20-day return is negative in more than 50% of months when both PAYEMS and INDPRO increase month-over-month, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound environment (FEDFUNDS near 0.16%), the federal funds rate has no further room to ease, so low rates no longer provide positive impetus to equity markets; instead, the persistence of near-zero rates signals economic weakness that weighs on stocks.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.16% is near zero lower bound
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 5-day return is positive in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates sudden consumer spending contraction, which often foreshadows economic slowdown and triggers immediate negative market reaction within a week.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 5-day return is positive in more than 30% of instances where RSAFS declines more than 1% month-over-month, the hypothesis fails.
